content.text: is a mode that gives Claude extra reasoning time before it answers, so it can work through harder problems more carefully instead of replying too quickly.[]

When extended thinking is on, Claude spends additional internal reasoning effort on the problem before producing its final response. That usually improves results on tasks that have many trade-offs, hidden dependencies, or multi-step logic.[]
that depend on state or edge cases.
where non-obvious issues may be easy to miss.[]
It is less useful for simple, routine edits where speed matters more than deeper analysis.[]
How it fits with Plan Mode
what Claude is allowed to do
They solve different problems and can be used together: Plan Mode keeps Claude read-only, while Extended Thinking helps it reason more thoroughly inside that safe boundary.[]

The trade-off is simple: better reasoning usually means more latency and more thinking tokens. For complex coding work, that is often worth it because it reduces back-and-forth and helps Claude stay on track.[]

. For easy questions, that pause is unnecessary. For difficult engineering work, it can make the difference between a shallow answer and a careful one.[]

It is best for complex, multi-step, or ambiguous technical problems.[]
It costs more time and tokens than a normal response.[]
It complements Plan Mode, but it is not the same thing.[]
